416	`presheafHom F G` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`) sends an open `W` to the morphisms of
417	the restrictions of `F`, `G` to the slice `Over W`.  Its value at `U i` is built from the
418	components of `f i`, conjugated by `eqToHom` along the down-set identity
419	`(U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` (valid for `V ≤ U i`).  The naturality field — the genuine
420	coherence risk — is automatic on the thin poset `Opens X` once the `eqToHom`-conjugation is
421	peeled, via `Subsingleton.elim` on the hom-sets. -/
422	noncomputable def homLocalSection {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type*}
423	    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι) :
424	    (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf).obj (op (U i)) where
425	  app W :=
426	    haveI hle : W.unop.left ≤ U i := W.unop.hom.le
427	    haveI himg : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left) = W.unop.left := by
428	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
429	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle
430	    M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg.symm)) ≫
431	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫
432	      N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg))
433	  naturality := by
434	    intro A B φ
435	    have hBA : (unop B).left ≤ (unop A).left := ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).le
436	    let κ : (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left ⟶ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left :=
437	      (Opens.map (U i).ι.base).map (homOfLE hBA)
438	    have himgA : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left) = (unop A).left := by
439	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
440	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop A).hom.le
441	    have himgB : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left) = (unop B).left := by
442	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
443	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop B).hom.le
444	    -- naturality of the underlying ab-presheaf morphism of `f i`
445	    have hm := ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).naturality κ.op
446	    -- the two thin-poset square edges agree (`Opens X` is a thin poset)
447	    have hsubM : ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm)
448	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm) ≫ ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op :=
449	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
450	    have hsubN : ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB)
451	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA) ≫ ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op :=
452	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
453	    -- M-side: the φ-restriction followed by the `eqToHom` is the `eqToHom` followed by `κ`
454	    have hML : M.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫
455	          M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm))
456	        = M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm)) ≫
457	          (M.restrict (U i).ι).val.presheaf.map κ.op := by
458	      rw [(M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubM]
459	      exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
460	    -- N-side analogue
461	    have hNR : N.val.presheaf.map ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫
462	          N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB))
463	        = N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA)) ≫
464	          N.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op := by
465	      rw [(N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubN]
466	      exact N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
467	    dsimp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.op_map, Functor.op_obj, Functor.comp_obj]
468	    rw [← Category.assoc, hML]
469	    erw [Category.assoc, reassoc_of% hm, hNR]
470	    simp only [Category.assoc]
471	    rfl

476	at `op ⊤` already determines a full compatible family of sections (each open's value is the
477	restriction of the top section), which `presheafHomSectionsEquiv` identifies with a morphism
478	`F ⟶ G`.  This is sub-step (b) of `homOfLocalCompat`. -/
479	noncomputable def topSectionToHom {X : TopCat.{u}}
480	    {F G : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}}
481	    (s : (CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G).obj (op ⊤)) : F ⟶ G :=
482	  CategoryTheory.presheafHomSectionsEquiv F G
483	    ⟨fun W => (CategoryTheory.presheafHom F G).map (homOfLE le_top).op s, by
484	      intro W W' e
485	      dsimp only
486	      rw [← Functor.map_comp_apply]

496	  obtain ⟨W⟩ := W
497	  exact CategoryTheory.presheafHom_map_app_op_mk_id (homOfLE le_top) s

505	  simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]

512	Blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` (~L5592).  Let `X` be a scheme,
513	`M N : X.Modules`, and `{U i}` an indexed open cover of `X`.  If for each `i` we have a
514	morphism `f i : M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι` in `Scheme.Modules (U i)` such
515	that the underlying section maps of `f i` and `f j` agree, *sectionwise*, on every open
516	`V ≤ U i ⊓ U j` (each conjugated into the fixed abelian-group hom-type `M(V) ⟶ N(V)` by the
517	canonical `eqToHom`s from the down-set identity `ι(ι⁻¹V) = V`), then there is a unique global
518	morphism `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U i` is `f i`.

520	The compatibility hypothesis `hf` is the **sectionwise** overlap-agreement (iter-254 re-sign;
521	this `def` is NOT in `archon-protected.yaml` and has no compiling caller, so the prover owns its
522	signature).  The earlier `HEq` form — comparing the two `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-images of
523	`f i`, `f j` along the two slice-restrictions — was *unsatisfiable*: those images live in
524	sheafifications of pullback presheaves along *different* morphisms, hence in only-isomorphic
525	(not propositionally equal) objects, so no `HEq`-elimination applies and no caller can produce
526	the datum.  The sectionwise form compares only the section maps, which live in the fixed group
527	`M(V) ⟶ N(V)`, and is exactly the data a caller (two local morphisms literally agreeing on
528	overlaps) has at hand.  See blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` sub-step (a).

682	further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
683	noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
684	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
685	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
686	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
687	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ
688	
689	/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
690	`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
691	lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
692	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
693	    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
694	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
695	  ext1 X; rfl
696	
697	/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
698	lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
699	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
700	    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
701	  ext1 X; rfl
702	
703	/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*
704	objects agrees up to `HEq`. Proven by `subst`. Used to discharge the
705	pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in
706	its argument up to `Over.mapId_eq` / `Over.mapComp_eq`). -/
707	private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
708	    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
709	  subst h; rfl
710	
711	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
712	lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
713	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
714	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
715	    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
716	  ext1 X
717	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))
718	
719	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
720	restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
721	lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
722	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
723	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
724	    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
725	  ext1 X
726	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))
727	
728	/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
729	`pushforward₀.map`). -/
730	lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
731	    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
732	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
733	    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
734	  Functor.map_comp _ _ _
735	
736	/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
737	and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
738	`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
739	of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
740	both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
741	functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
742	lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
743	    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
744	    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
745	    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
746	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
747	      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
748	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
749	  ext1 Y
750	  ext m
751	  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
752	  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
753	  congr 1
754	  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
755	  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
756	        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
757	      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
758	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
759	    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
760	      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
761	    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
762	        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
763	    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
764	    rw [e1, e2]
765	    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
766	  rw [hmor]
767	  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
768	  rfl
769	
770	/-- **`restrictionMap` packaged as an additive group homomorphism** on the morphism
771	groups, using `restrictionMap_zero` and `restrictionMap_add`. This is the value of the
772	underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
773	noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
774	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
775	    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
776	  toFun := restrictionMap g
777	  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
778	  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g
779	
780	/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
781	with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
782	`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
783	at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
784	noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
785	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
786	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
787	  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
788	  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
789	  map_id X := by
790	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
791	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
792	    exact restrictionMap_id φ
793	  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
794	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
795	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
796	    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ
797	
798	/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
799	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
800	`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
801	R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
802	actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
803	compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
804	`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
805	lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
806	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
807	    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
808	    restrictionMap f.unop
809	        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
810	      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
811	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
812	  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
813	    = restrictionMap f.unop m
814	        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
815	  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]
816	
817	end InternalHom
818	
819	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
820	
821	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
822	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
823	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
824	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
825	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
826	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
827	
828	namespace InternalHom
829	
830	section Assembly
831	
832	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
833	
834	/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):
835	the presheaf of `R`-modules assembled from the slice value modules
836	`internalHomObjModule` (a), the further-restriction maps `internalHomPresheaf` (b)(c),
837	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
838	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
839	section note for the universe constraint. -/
840	noncomputable def internalHom
841	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
842	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
843	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
844	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
845	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
846	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
847	
848	end Assembly
849	
850	end InternalHom
851	
852	namespace InternalHom
853	
854	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
855	
856	universe vC uC
857	
858	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
859	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
860	
861	/-- **`termRingMap` at the terminal object itself is the identity.** The unique
862	`T → T` is the identity, so the induced ring map `R(T) → R(T)` is `id`. This is the
863	fact that the global-scalar action evaluated at the terminal section is the scalar
864	itself, used to prove the evaluation map of `internalHomEval` is `R(U)`-bilinear. -/
865	lemma termRingMap_terminal (f : (R.obj (op T) : Type u)) :
866	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT (op T))) f = f := by
867	  have hid : hT.from T = 𝟙 T := hT.hom_ext _ _
868	  simp only [termRingMap, unop_op, hid, op_id, R.map_id]
869	  rfl
870	
871	end InternalHom
872	
873	/-! ### The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`
874	
875	The presheaf dual is the internal hom into the monoidal unit (the structure presheaf
876	viewed as a module over itself), assembled in the same single-universe context as
877	`InternalHom.internalHom`. Blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`. -/
878	
879	section Dual
880	
881	open InternalHom Opposite
882	
883	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
884	
885	/-- **The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`): the
886	internal hom into the monoidal unit `𝟙_` (the structure presheaf `R` viewed as a module
887	over itself / the regular representation). The value
888	`M^∨(U) = ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ R|_U)` is the `R(U)`-module of `R|_U`-linear
889	functionals on `M|_U`, the presheaf-of-modules analogue of the fixed-ring linear dual
890	`Module.Dual R M = (M ⟶ R)` that serves as the `⊗`-inverse of an invertible module.
891	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring dual but no `PresheafOfModules`-level dual for
892	the varying structure sheaf. -/
893	noncomputable def dual
894	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
895	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
896	  InternalHom.internalHom M (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
897	
898	/-- The evaluation functional `φ ↦ (s ↦ φ(s))` of a dual section `φ`, cast to an
899	`R₀.obj X`-linear map `M(X) → R(X)` (the over-category ring at the terminal section is
900	definitionally `R₀.obj X`). Helper for `internalHomEvalApp`. -/
901	noncomputable def evalLin
902	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
903	    (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
904	    (M.obj X : Type u) →ₗ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] (R₀.obj X : Type u) :=
905	  ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
906	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
907	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
908	
909	/-- `evalLin` is additive in the dual section `φ` (the dual-section addition is the
910	categorical `Hom`-addition, on which `app`/`hom`-application is definitionally additive). -/
911	lemma evalLin_add
912	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
913	    (φ φ' : (dual M).obj X) :
914	    evalLin M X (φ + φ') = evalLin M X φ + evalLin M X φ' :=
915	  LinearMap.ext fun _ => rfl
916	
917	/-- `evalLin` is `R₀.obj X`-linear in the dual section `φ`, using the `homModule`
918	action `c • φ = φ ≫ globalSMul c` and `termRingMap_terminal`. -/
919	lemma evalLin_smul
920	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
921	    (c : (R₀.obj X : Type u)) (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
922	    evalLin M X (c • φ) = c • evalLin M X φ := by
923	  refine LinearMap.ext fun s => ?_
924	  rw [LinearMap.smul_apply]
925	  change ((ConcreteCategory.hom
926	      (termRingMap (R := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀) Over.mkIdTerminal
927	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))) c)
928	    • ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
929	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
930	          (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom s : (R₀.obj X : Type u))
931	    = c • evalLin M X φ s
932	  rw [termRingMap_terminal]
933	  rfl
934	
935	/-- **The open-by-open evaluation/contraction map** underlying `internalHomEval`. At an
936	object `X` it is the `R(X)`-bilinear contraction
937	`(M(X)) ⊗_{R(X)} (M|_X ⟶ R|_X) → R(X)`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, where `φ(s)` evaluates the
938	functional `φ` (a morphism of restricted presheaves of modules over `Over X.unop`) at its
939	terminal section, applied to `s`. Bilinearity over `R(X)`: linearity in `s` is linearity
940	of `evalLin`; linearity in `φ` is `evalLin_add` / `evalLin_smul`. The codomain is cast to
941	`R₀.obj X` (defeq to the unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`) to pin the `CommRingCat` module. -/
942	noncomputable def internalHomEvalApp
943	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
944	    (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M)).obj X ⟶
945	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj X :=
946	  show ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u)
947	        (TensorProduct (R₀.obj X : Type u) (M.obj X) ((dual M).obj X))
948	      ⟶ ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u) (R₀.obj X : Type u) from
949	  ModuleCat.ofHom
950	    (TensorProduct.lift
951	      (LinearMap.mk₂ (R₀.obj X : Type u)
952	        (fun (s : M.obj X) (φ : (dual M).obj X) => evalLin M X φ s)
953	        (fun s s' φ => _root_.map_add (evalLin M X φ) s s')
954	        (fun c s φ => _root_.map_smul (evalLin M X φ) c s)
955	        (fun s φ φ' => by
956	          change evalLin M X (φ + φ') s = evalLin M X φ s + evalLin M X φ' s
957	          rw [evalLin_add, LinearMap.add_apply])
958	        (fun c s φ => by
959	          change evalLin M X (c • φ) s = c • evalLin M X φ s
960	          rw [evalLin_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply])))
961	
962	/-- Value of `internalHomEvalApp` on a simple tensor: the contraction `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`.
963	The eval value is kept at its NATURAL over-ring type `R₀.obj X` (not ascribed to the
964	unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`). Used to reduce the naturality of `internalHomEval`. -/
965	@[simp] lemma internalHomEvalApp_tmul
966	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
967	    (s : (M.obj X : Type u)) (φ : ((dual M).obj X : Type u)) :
968	    (internalHomEvalApp M X).hom (s ⊗ₜ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] φ) = evalLin M X φ s :=
969	  rfl
970	
971	/-- **Further-restriction of `M|_U` along the canonical `Over.homMk f.unop` equals `M.map f`.**
972	For `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Dᵒᵖ`, restricting `M|_{X.unop}` (the `pushforward₀` of `M` along
973	`Over.forget X.unop`) along the over-category morphism `Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶
974	Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)` is, definitionally, `M.map f`. Extracted as its own lemma so the heavy
975	`whnf` defeq runs once within its own heartbeat budget (the `internalHomEval` naturality proof
976	would otherwise time out). Used to rewrite the `naturality_apply` of a dual section into the
977	shape `ev_M`'s naturality needs. -/
978	private lemma restr_map_homMk
979	    (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
980	    (restr X.unop N).map (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
981	      = N.map f := rfl
982	
983	/-- **The evaluation morphism `ev_M : M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`** (blueprint `lem:internal_hom_eval`):
984	the full natural morphism `M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, with `app X := internalHomEvalApp M X`.
985	CLOSED axiom-clean iter-224 (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`). The per-object/value content
986	is `internalHomEvalApp` + `internalHomEvalApp_tmul`; the `naturality` field is the six-step
987	`evalLin`/`key`/`hdt` reduction recorded in the proof body. The iter-222/223 `whnf` HEARTBEAT-BOMB
988	diagnosis (>200000 heartbeats forced by `kabstract` whnf-ing the monoidal unit `𝟙_`) turned out to be
989	STALE after a Mathlib update: the composition now splits cleanly with
990	`erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]`, every elementwise rewrite (`internalHomEvalApp_tmul`,
991	`PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply`, `restr_map_homMk`) fires without any bomb, and the dual-section
992	naturality square closes directly. No transparency hacks (`with_reducible`), no `unit`-reshape, and no
993	`maxHeartbeats` bump were needed. -/
994	noncomputable def internalHomEval
995	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
996	    PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M) ⟶
997	      𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
998	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk (fun X => internalHomEvalApp M X) (by
999	    intro X Y f
1000	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)
1001	    -- iter-224: the iter-222/223 `whnf` HEARTBEAT-BOMB diagnosis (the codomain `𝟙_` forcing
1002	    -- `kabstract` to whnf the monoidal-unit machinery, >200000 heartbeats) turned out STALE after a
1003	    -- Mathlib update. The composition splits cleanly with `erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, …]` and every
1004	    -- elementwise rewrite fires without any bomb; the six-step `evalLin`/`key`/`hdt` reduction below
1005	    -- goes through directly. No `with_reducible`, no `unit`-reshape, no `maxHeartbeats` bump.
1006	    -- Step 1: break the two `≫` on `s ⊗ₜ φ`, then `tensorObj_map_tmul` + `internalHomEvalApp_tmul`
1007	    -- (handled by `erw`'s defeq matching through `restrictScalars`):
1008	    --   LHS ⇒ `evalLin M Y ((dual M).map f φ) ((M.map f) s)`.
1009	    erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply,
1010	      internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]
1011	    simp only []
1012	    -- Reduce the RHS `(𝟙_).map f`-image of `(internalHomEvalApp M X)(s ⊗ₜ φ)` to the unit ring
1013	    -- map applied to `evalLin M X φ s` (defeq, since `internalHomEvalApp_tmul` is `rfl`).
1014	    change M.evalLin Y ((M.dual.map f) φ) ((M.map f) s)
1015	      = ((𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
1016	          (M.evalLin X φ s)
1017	    -- Step 4: naturality of the dual section `φ` (a morphism over `Over X.unop`) along the
1018	    -- canonical `(Over.homMk f.unop).op : (terminal) ⟶ (op (Over.mk f.unop))`, applied to `s`.

271	
272	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
273	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
274	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
275	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
276	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
277	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
278	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
279	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/

288	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
289	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
290	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
291	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
292	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
293	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
294	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)

input.content:
# Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean

**Iter 258. Status: HELD (per plan) — stale comments cleaned; file compiles, 2 typed sorries retained.**

This file was explicitly **HELD this iter** in PROGRESS.md / task_pending.md (Lane TS-inv),
gated on the shared root `Scheme.Modules.overEquivalence` (`Picard/SheafOverEquivalence.lean`),
which is being built in a parallel lane and still has 3 open sorries. The plan's sole sanctioned
action for this file this iter was: "stale header/comments (aud257 must-fix L18/L207/L639) to be
cleaned when re-opened." I did exactly that, plus an empirical confirmation of the remaining
obligation, and verified the file compiles cleanly.

## What I changed (comment-only; no proof terms touched)

1. **Header docstring (piece 1 / new piece 1b).** Updated `dual_restrict_iso`'s "PARTIAL
   (iter-251)" description; added an explicit `sliceDualTransport` (piece 1b) entry stating the
   exact remaining obligation and that it is the **per-open shadow of `Opens.overEquivalence`**,
   i.e. closes as a *consumer* of the shared root, not via the hand-rolled sectionwise build.
2. **`sliceDualTransport` body comment.** Retired the false "concurrently-broken
   `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` dependency" excuse (TensorObjSubstrate compiles; this file builds with
   only the 2 typed sorries). Recorded the empirical probe result (below) and the consumer route.
3. **`homOfLocalCompat` sub-step (c) comment.** Removed the stale "the SOLE remaining `sorry` is
   the inner ring-bridge" claim — `homOfLocalCompat` is **CLOSED axiom-clean** (verified:
   `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`), so the comment now says CLOSED and names the
   `hbridge`/`hfl_native` mechanism.

## Empirical confirmation of the `sliceDualTransport` wall

### sliceDualTransport (now L184)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Probe `refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso ?_` at the sorry to see the reduced goal.
- **Result:** PARTIAL (diagnostic) — `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso` cleanly reduces the goal to a
  `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear equivalence
  `(restr fV' M.val ⟶ restr fV' 𝟙_X) ≃ₗ[𝒪_Y(V)] (restr V ((pushforward β) M.val) ⟶ restr V 𝟙_Y)`
  (`fV' = f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`). The two `ModuleCat` module instances synthesize, so the
  `≃ₗ` goal is well-posed.
- **Key finding:** This `≃ₗ` is **exactly the per-open localization of `Opens.overEquivalence`**
  at `V` — the same construction the shared root `Scheme.Modules.overEquivalence` provides at the
  sheaf level. Building it directly is a ~200 LOC sectionwise dual-section reindexing (forward map
  = `homLocalSection`-style component conjugation by `f.appIso` + `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`
  on the terminal value; inverse via the down-set bijection; thin-poset naturality). This is the
  multi-iter churn the plan deliberately pivoted away from.
- **Decision:** Did NOT churn the throwaway sectionwise build. Per the plan, this closes NEXT iter
  as a one-liner consuming `overEquivalence` once the shared root lands. Typed `sorry` retained at
  the intended signature (NOT advanced to a `≃ₗ` sorry, since the planned closing move replaces the
  whole body via the shared root, not fills the `≃ₗ`).

### dual_restrict_iso (now L316)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** The `isoMk` naturality residual (Step 4 assembly). Gated on `sliceDualTransport`.
- **Result:** BLOCKED — the `isoMk` naturality square cannot be discharged while
  `sliceDualTransport.hom` is opaque (`sorry`); it is genuine module-hom data, not
  `Subsingleton.elim`-collapsible. Closes once `sliceDualTransport` is concrete.

## Recommendation for the planner / next iter

- Keep DualInverse HELD until `Scheme.Modules.overEquivalence` (+ `restrictOverIso`/`unitOverIso`)
  land in `Picard/SheafOverEquivalence.lean`.
- Then the closing move for `sliceDualTransport`: obtain the per-`V` `≃ₗ` from `overEquivalence`
  localized to `V` (the functor underlying `(overEquivalence U).functor` is `pushforward φ`; its
  sectionwise action + `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` give precisely the dual-section transport).
  Verify whether the presheaf-vs-sheaf level mismatch flagged historically in
  `ts230-cbridge-outcome-ii` is in fact dissolved by the new root (the new root is a genuine
  `SheafOfModules` equivalence with a `pushforward φ` functor, unlike the old fixed-value-cat
  `overSliceSheafEquiv`). If the root's functor does not localize to the presheaf `≃ₗ` cleanly,
  the fallback is the documented ~200 LOC sectionwise build (homLocalSection + ring transport).
- An import of `Picard/SheafOverEquivalence.lean` will need to be added when re-opened (creates the
  cross-lane dependency the plan held to avoid this iter).

## Blueprint markers
- No declarations newly closed → no new `\leanok` to flag. `homOfLocalCompat` (CLOSED iter-256)
  and the axiom-clean `presheafDualUnitIso`/`dual_unit_iso`/`dualUnitIsoGen` are already handled by
  the deterministic `sync_leanok`. No marker changes requested from review.
